hero.dataTypes: GPS data. Strength data. Physio notes. Biomechanical measurements. Neuromuscular signals.
hero.valueProp: Twinspire builds a model that adapts as the athlete changes, so every decision is made against that player's own evolving baseline.
hero.credibility: Built on five years of DTU research.

team.heading: The Research Came First.
team.headingAccent: The Team Was Built Around It.

descriptions.pouya: Pouya played professional football until a reinjury forced him to take a break from the pitch. The signals were there. Nothing in the process had the tools to read them. He returned to DTU, turned that experience into a research question, and built what the sport was missing. His MSc in Electrical Engineering, specialising in AI and control systems, became the foundation for Twinspire's core methodology and the basis for the patent. He leads research translation, strategic direction, and external partnerships.
descriptions.john: John is a Professor at DTU and has spent years leading research into digital twin methodology for physiological systems - work that forms the scientific foundation of Twinspire. His neuromuscular research group is where the project was born, and his lab remains its institutional anchor. Beyond research, John has built and sold technology companies with exits to Facebook, Oculus, and GN Audio, bringing a rare combination of scientific depth and entrepreneurial experience to the team.
descriptions.daryan: Daryan has spent seven years building software at the intersection of architecture, data engineering, and people leadership. At Twinspire he leads the technical implementation that connects club data sources, the app and web infrastructure that practitioners use daily. He has led development teams and mentored engineers throughout his career.
descriptions.roxane: Roxane has built and taken AI ventures to market across three continents. She previously co-founded CUJU, an AI sports scouting company developed in close partnership with ROGON Sports Management, using computer vision to identify talent in the Global South, taking it from zero to exit. Before that she worked with the UN and World Bank on innovation programme development, and has evaluated and supported 100+ early-stage ventures. She holds an M.Sc. and CEMS Master's from CBS and NUS, and two Executive Degrees from MIT Sloan. At Twinspire she leads fundraising, partnerships, and the commercial strategy that takes the platform from research validation to market.
descriptions.ali: Ali has a solid background in machine learning and applied analytics, with experience building ML pipelines and data-driven solutions. His work includes research in anomaly detection and predictive quality using complex industrial datasets, reflecting his interest in practical AI applications and intelligent systems that solve real-world problems and create meaningful impact. He holds an MSc in Computer Engineering from Aarhus University.

builtFor.heading: Who We
builtFor.subtitle: We are currently reaching out to clubs, clinics, and performance institutions to participate in our research validation study.

footballers.title: Footballers
footballers.description: Your body has been generating data your whole career. Most of it has disappeared. This research aims to establish whether a structured data passport can change that and give you ownership of your own athletic history.

coaches.title: Coaches & Performance Staff
coaches.description: Help us understand how readiness modeling integrates into real decision-making environments, and where the model's outputs are and aren't actionable.

physio.title: Physiotherapists
physio.description: Co-develop unified deviation-alert and recovery-signal workflows grounded in multi-source data. Help us understand what a clinically useful signal actually looks like in practice.

science.label: The Science
science.heading: Most systems describe load.
science.headingAccent: Twinspire is being developed to model individual response.
science.intro: The research draws on principles from adaptive systems, time-varying dynamics, and individualized state estimation to study how each athlete responds across time. Rather than treating the athlete as a static profile or comparing them against population averages, the objective is to identify personalized reference behavior and detect meaningful departures from that expected state.

modeling.title: Individualized Modeling
modeling.body: The methodology investigates how multimodal data can be used to identify adaptable mathematical models of the individual athlete that capture changing physiological, neuromuscular, and performance-related dynamics over time.

signals.title: Multi-Signal Analysis
signals.body: Alongside individualized modeling, Twinspire is investigating larger-scale relationships among signals across integrated partner data streams to determine which combinations of measures provide the strongest explanatory power. This includes ongoing and planned research collaborations spanning performance monitoring, rehabilitation environments, neuromuscular sensing, and cognitive and mental load-related assessments.

validation.title: Validation & Risk Reduction
validation.body: The current phase focuses on methodological validation, resolution of key scientific uncertainties, and reduction of the technical risks required before broader applied deployment in performance and rehabilitation settings.

science.researchTitle: The current work addresses three questions:

questions.q1: How early can deviations from an athlete's personal baseline be detected, and which deviations are meaningfully associated with elevated vulnerability following return to play?
questions.q2: What is the minimum sensor and data configuration required to establish a stable and reliable individualized model?
questions.q3: How do missing periods, changing contexts, and club transitions affect model robustness over time?
questions.q4: Can emerging compensation patterns be identified before they become evident through symptoms or conventional monitoring?

trust.body: GDPR-compliant. EU servers. Never sold. Never shared. The passport belongs to the athlete. The squad data belongs to the club. We host it — we don't own it.
trust.disclaimer: Twinspire is a decision support platform and is not intended to diagnose or treat medical conditions.

product.headline1: From Population Averages to Individualized Dynamics
product.p1: The approach combines principles from computational motor control, adaptive nonlinear systems, system identification, longitudinal sequence modelling, and self supervised learning to identify individualized representations of how an athlete’s physiological and functional state changes over time.

card1.title: Digital twin framework
card1.body: Twinspire is developing a research-based digital twin framework for individualized athlete modeling. The system integrates longitudinal data from multiple sources, including training load, neuromuscular testing, clinician input, and wearable-derived physiological measures.

card2.title: Approach
card2.body: The approach combines principles from computational motor control, adaptive nonlinear systems, system identification, longitudinal sequence modelling, and self supervised learning to identify individualized representations of how an athlete’s physiological and functional state changes over time.

card3.title: Structured data layer
card3.body: In parallel, Twinspire is building a structured data layer that links sessions, tests, symptoms, and contextual information into a portable athlete history, supporting continuity and decision-making across performance and rehabilitation.

product.headline2: From Research Prototype to Applied Validation
product.p2: A research prototype has been developed across mobile and web environments, designed to integrate heterogeneous data with minimal input from practitioners. The system now enters real-world validation, focusing on whether it remains usable, robust, and interpretable within elite performance settings.

questions.q1: Are workflows operationally usable for staff?
questions.q2: Do data pipelines provide sufficient quality and continuity?
questions.q3: Do the signals support meaningful individualized modeling in practice?

product.mockupAlt: Twinspire staff dashboard and player mobile app showing simulation parameters and individualized readiness metrics

problem.label: The Problem
problem.headline: You're Making Sunday's Decision with Tuesday's Data.
problem.p1: GPS in one platform. Strength data in another. Clinical notes remain local. Wearable signals often never leave the athlete’s own device. The challenge in elite sport is no longer access to data alone, but the absence of a coherent longitudinal model of the individual athlete across contexts and over time.
problem.p2: Football injury epidemiology is well established through long-running cohort studies, including the UEFA Elite Club research, which spans thousands of players across multiple countries and seasons. Yet despite increasingly sophisticated monitoring environments, the central decision problem remains unresolved: how to interpret changing signals at the individual level when decisions about return to training, return to play, and load modification must be made under uncertainty.
problem.p3: Subsequent and recurrent injuries continue to represent a meaningful part of the burden in professional football, and the period following return remains especially sensitive. The gap is therefore not simply one of data collection, but of individualized interpretation.
problem.imageAlt: Coaching staff analyzing player performance data on the sideline
problem.card2ImageAlt: Footballers training on the pitch during a competitive session
problem.originBody: Twinspire began with a rehabilitation process that, on paper, followed the expected path: monitoring, testing, staged progression, and medical clearance. Yet a second injury followed shortly after return. That experience exposed a broader research gap: even in data-rich elite environments, critical decisions are still made based on fragmented data and without a robust individualized model of the athlete across time. Twinspire was founded to resolve that gap.
problem.founderAlt: Twinspire founder during rehabilitation — the experience that inspired the research

cta.title: We Are Selecting
cta.subtitle: Clubs and clinics that join the research study contribute directly to shaping the methodology, gain early insight into interim findings, and help ensure the approach is grounded in real-world clinical and performance practice before any market introduction is considered. If your work or technology is relevant to our research, we would like to hear from you!
cta.fields: Name · Club or Clinic · Role · Email
cta.note: No commitment. A short conversation to explore whether the collaboration is a good fit.

partners.heading: Build the Future of Football Health With Us.
partners.body: We work with sensor providers, clinics, and research institutions pushing the boundaries of performance and rehabilitation. If your work belongs in the stack — let's talk.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Answers to the most common questions about Twinspire, our methodology, and how clubs can get started.

whatIsTwinspire.question: What is Twinspire?
whatIsTwinspire.answer: Twinspire is an AI-powered decision support platform for football clubs. It builds a continuously updated physiological model of each player by drawing on GPS data, strength output, neuromuscular sensor data, physio notes, and the athlete's own wearables, and alerts medical and coaching staff when something shifts. The goal is to catch the warning signs before an injury becomes a reinjury.

whatProblemDoesTwinspireSolve.question: What problem does Twinspire solve?
whatProblemDoesTwinspireSolve.answer: One in five footballers tears the same muscle twice. Not from bad luck, but from missed signals. The reinjury rate in football hasn't moved in a decade, largely because the tools available to staff don't connect all the relevant data in one place. GPS lives in one system. Strength test results in another. Wearable data never leaves the player's phone. Twinspire is built to close that gap and give staff a single, objective picture of each player's readiness.

whoIsTwinspireFor.question: Who is Twinspire for?
whoIsTwinspireFor.answer: Twinspire is built for three groups: physiotherapists who need unified deviation alerts and recovery signals across all data sources; coaches and performance staff who need to know a squad's true readiness before setting a lineup; and footballers themselves, who finally have a place to store their career-long data.

howDoesTwinspireWork.question: How does Twinspire actually work?
howDoesTwinspireWork.answer: Twinspire learns what normal looks like for each individual body. It fuses data from wearables, EMG signals, GPS-based movement tracking, and clinical assessments to build a living physiological model unique to each athlete. Once a baseline is established, the platform detects deviations - small shifts in neuromuscular output, recovery patterns, or load response - and surfaces them to staff before they become injuries.

whatIsAthleticPassport.question: What is the Athletic Passport?
whatIsAthleticPassport.answer: The Athletic Passport is a structured, portable record of everything a player's body has done. Every session, every load, every injury, every rehab milestone. It belongs to the athlete, not the club. When a player moves teams, their full history moves with them. For clubs, it means inheriting objective data on every new signing rather than starting from scratch.

isTwinspireDashboard.question: Is Twinspire a dashboard?
isTwinspireDashboard.answer: No. Most platforms count load. Twinspire understands response. It is not a data visualization layer on top of existing tools - it is a predictive model. The methodology is patent-pending, built on 18 months of real-world data, and validated with athletes from 3rd- to 1st-Division clubs in Denmark.

whatIsDigitalTwin.question: What is a digital twin?
whatIsDigitalTwin.answer: A digital twin is a continuously updated AI model of a real-world subject. In this case, an individual athlete. Rather than comparing a player against a generic population benchmark, a digital twin learns what is normal for that specific body: how it responds to load, how quickly it recovers, what its baseline neuromuscular output looks like on a good day. Once a baseline is established, the model monitors for deviations. A shift that might look unremarkable in a squad-wide average can be a significant warning sign for a particular player. The term originates in engineering, where digital twins are used to simulate and monitor physical assets such as aircraft engines and power grids. Twinspire applies the same principle to human physiology by treating each athlete as a system to be modeled individually, not just tracked collectively.

whatDataDoesTwinspireUse.question: What data does Twinspire use?
whatDataDoesTwinspireUse.answer: Twinspire draws on GPS and movement data, strength and neuromuscular output, EMG muscle signals, clinical and physio assessments, and the athlete's own wearables - including data generated away from the training ground. All data streams are normalized through an automated ingestion pipeline. If you have something that is not listed, do not hesitate to reach out.

whoOwnsData.question: Who owns the data?
whoOwnsData.answer: The data belongs to the people who generate it. The athlete owns their Athletic Passport. The club owns its squad data. Twinspire hosts it - it does not own it, and it is never sold or shared with third parties. Processing is GDPR-compliant and EU-based.

isTwinspireGdprCompliant.question: Is Twinspire GDPR compliant?
isTwinspireGdprCompliant.answer: Yes. Twinspire is built with GDPR compliance and EU-based data hosting from the ground up. Data processing agreements are available for clubs and clinics. The platform includes granular access controls so athletes, medical staff, and coaches each see only what is relevant to them.

whereIsTwinspireBased.question: Where is Twinspire based, and who built it?
whereIsTwinspireBased.answer: Twinspire was founded at the Technical University of Denmark (DTU) in Copenhagen. The company was started by Pouya Tobias Strand Nikoui, a former professional footballer who suffered a reinjury after a full clinical clearance, and went back to DTU to build what didn't exist. The team combines expertise in AI and product, platform architecture, scientific research, and data security, backed by DTU Skylab and DIF Innovation Lab.

howToGetStarted.question: How do I get started?
howToGetStarted.answer: Twinspire is currently onboarding founding partner clubs across the Nordics. There is no commitment to apply - just a short call to see if it's the right fit. You can reach out via the contact form on the website or directly at info@twinspire.ai.

dataEthics.heading: Athlete Data Must Be Handled
dataEthics.subtitle: How we approach data governance at every stage of research.
dataEthics.description: Twinspire is being developed with data governance and research ethics as core design principles.
dataEthics.card1: The current phase is focused on scientific validation, with attention to lawful processing, traceability, transparency, and responsible handling of longitudinal athlete data.
dataEthics.card2: The project is built around GDPR-aligned data practices, EU-based hosting and processing, relevant data processing agreements, and research workflows designed to support clear governance across clubs, practitioners, and athletes. Where appropriate, research data management is structured in accordance with FAIR principles.
dataEthics.card3: Twinspire is a research-stage decision support prototype. It is not intended to diagnose, treat, or replace clinical judgment.
dataEthics.disclaimer: Twinspire is a research-stage decision support prototype. It is not intended to diagnose, treat, or replace clinical judgment.

joinUs.pageSubtitle: We are building a founding research cohort of clubs, clinics, and performance institutions. Apply to participate in our validation study.
joinUs.heroSubtitle: We believe exceptional products are built by exceptional people.

intro.p1: At Twinspire, we're developing a digital twin platform for individualised athlete modelling in elite football. Our work sits at the intersection of sports science, data, and performance environments where outcomes matter. Decisions are made under uncertainty, often with incomplete information. That's the context we build for.
intro.p2: We are a small team with a broad range of experience, from early 20s to mid-60s. What unites us is a shared approach: we take ownership, we focus on execution, and we prefer clarity over complexity. There is little interest here in unnecessary process or work that doesn't translate into something tangible.
intro.p3: There's often a perceived trade-off between experience and willingness to build. We don't think that holds. Some people naturally stay close to the work, regardless of seniority. Those are the people we value — individuals who take responsibility, contribute directly, and care about what they produce.
intro.p4: We work out of DTU in Copenhagen, with the flexibility to work from home. We operate with a high level of trust and expect people to manage their time accordingly. We are an international and diverse team, and we see that as a strength.
intro.p5: What you can expect is meaningful ownership, a direct connection between your work and its impact, and an environment where things move without unnecessary friction. We are early, and we are ambitious, which practically means that you'll have the opportunity to shape both the product and how it is being built.
intro.p6: If this sounds like the kind of place you want to contribute, we'd like to hear from you. If you don't see an opening that fits you, reach out regardless. We are growing.

joinUsPage.value1Desc: Everyone is accountable for what they build and its outcomes.
joinUsPage.value2Desc: We move quickly and build things that have real impact.
joinUsPage.value3Desc: Simple, direct approaches over abstraction and unnecessary process.

joinUsPage.expect2Desc: We are early and ambitious, which means you'll have the opportunity to shape both the product and how it is being built.
joinUsPage.expect3Desc: An environment where things move without unnecessary friction, process, or bureaucracy.
joinUsPage.expect4Desc: Work from DTU in Copenhagen with flexibility to work from home. We trust people to manage their time.
joinUsPage.problemBody: One in five footballers re-injures the same muscle. That rate has not changed in a decade — not because the data is absent, but because the individual athlete has never been properly modelled. We are building the infrastructure to change that: physiological models specific to the person, not the population. The work is technical and the stakes are real. Practitioners make decisions under uncertainty, often with incomplete information. That is the context we design for.
joinUsPage.problemLead: One in five footballers tears the same muscle twice. The reinjury rate hasn't moved in a decade — not because the data doesn't exist, but because the individual athlete has never been modelled.
joinUsPage.problemPara1: We're an early team doing technical work in a space where the stakes are real — injuries, decisions, careers. We're not building dashboards. We're building something practitioners will trust under pressure.
joinUsPage.problemPara2: Decisions are made under uncertainty, often with incomplete information. That's the context we build for.
joinUsPage.ourTeamTitle: What does a professional footballer have in common with an engineer?
joinUsPage.ourTeamBody1: An irrational conviction that with the right system, failure becomes preventable. That's probably what holds us together.
joinUsPage.ourTeamBody2: We have a founder who lived the reinjury problem as a professional player and went back to DTU to build what the sport was missing. A professor whose research group has built and shipped digital twin systems for physiological modelling for years. A COO who co-founded and scaled a sports-tech venture and has worked with professional football players from around the world.
joinUsPage.ourTeamBody3: Between us all within the team, we hold more degrees than are probably necessary and speak Danish, English, French, Italian, Farsi, Arabic, Russian, Hungarian, Greek, and Icelandic, which says something about where we've come from, if not about how efficiently we run meetings. Different backgrounds, the same approach: take ownership and care about what you produce.
joinUsPage.ourTeamQuote: Different backgrounds, the same approach: take ownership and care about what you build

joinUsPage.hww3Desc: We are an international and diverse team, and we see that as a strength. We operate with a high level of trust and flexibility.
joinUsPage.expect5Desc: Your contributions will directly influence how elite athletes train and perform at the highest level.
joinUsPage.expect6Desc: Work at the cutting edge of sports science, data, and AI with opportunities to learn and grow.
joinUsPage.teamBody: We are a small, international team with a broad range of experience — from early 20s to mid-60s. What we share is a common approach: take ownership, stay close to the work, and care about what you produce.

physio.title: Physiological & Sports Medicine Advisor - Elite Football
physio.intro: At Twinspire, we're building a digital twin platform for individualised athlete modelling in elite football. We are already testing this live in clubs through ongoing pilot studies.
physio.question: The question we care about is simple: can data-driven signals actually support decisions that practitioners trust?
physio.theRoleDesc: We're looking for a physiotherapist, sports medicine professional, or physician with experience in elite or semi-elite football. Not to validate what we're building, but to challenge it.
physio.connectIntro: This is not a narrow return-to-play role. We're looking for someone who can connect:
physio.contextNote: You understand that athlete management is not clean or protocol-driven, but contextual, uncertain, and often subjective. That's exactly the gap we're trying to close.
physio.doLead: You will work directly with the founding team and DTU research group.
physio.do1: Pressure-test whether model outputs make sense physiologically
physio.do2: Call out when signals don't translate to real decisions
physio.do3: Help bridge the gap between research and applied practice
physio.do4: Shape what a useful signal actually looks like in elite environments
physio.do5: Push us to build something that holds up under scrutiny — not just in theory
physio.lf1: Has worked closely with elite or semi-elite football players
physio.lf2: Brings strong physiological and/or sports medicine expertise
physio.lf3: Is comfortable questioning both data and established practice
physio.lf4: Has felt the gap between what's measured and what's actually useful
physio.lf5: Wants to be part of defining what better looks like
physio.offer1: Direct involvement in a validation study with elite football clubs across the Nordics
physio.offer4: A chance to shape how athlete modelling is applied in real environments
physio.addressLine: DTU · Anker Engelunds Vej 1, 2800 Kongens Lyngby · www.twinspire.ai
physio.contactCta: If this resonates, reach out: roxane@twinspire.dk
physio.addressLineText: DTU · Anker Engelunds Vej 1, 2800 Kongens Lyngby ·

engineering.title: Engineering Lead - AI-Driven Data Platform
engineering.intro: Most AI products don't fail because of the models, but because the surrounding system isn't built to support real decisions. At Twinspire, we're building a digital twin platform for individualised athlete modelling in elite football. The modelling is advancing, and the platform is live in pilot environments.
engineering.nowChallenge: Now the real challenge begins: making the system robust, reliable, and usable in practice.
engineering.theRoleDesc: We're looking for an Engineering Lead to take ownership of the AI-driven data platform that underpins the product. This is a systems role.
engineering.messyData: You'll be working with messy, multi-source athlete data — aligning it into a consistent, reliable system where correctness, timing, and interpretability matter. The goal is perhaps simple to state, but hard to execute:
engineering.goal: turn data and model outputs into something practitioners can trust when making decisions.
engineering.leadIntro: You will lead the engineering effort across:
engineering.closelyCeo: You'll work closely with the CEO & founder, leading the modelling direction, and take full ownership of how that intelligence is operationalised in the product.
engineering.resp1: Turning a working stack into a robust, scalable product platform
engineering.resp2: Owning data correctness and consistency across athletes
engineering.resp3: Ensuring outputs are traceable, reliable, and usable
engineering.resp4: Leading and growing the engineering team
engineering.resp5c: onboarding new data sources and clubs
engineering.stack4: Data pipelines with idempotent ingestion and scheduled transformations
engineering.stack5: Integrations across multiple athlete data sources (Whoop, Statsports, Vald, etc.)
engineering.lf1: Has built and scaled data-heavy systems in production
engineering.lf2: Is comfortable across backend, data platforms, and cloud infrastructure
engineering.lf3: Thinks in systems — not just services
engineering.lf4: Cares about reliability, clarity, and correctness
engineering.lf5: Leads by taking ownership and staying hands-on
engineering.bonus1: messy, real-world data (sports, health, IoT, fintech)
engineering.hwt1: We believe exceptional products are built by exceptional people. There's often a perceived trade-off between experience and willingness to build. We don't think that's true.
engineering.hwt2: If you're a builder at heart, that doesn't go away — and that's who we're looking for.
engineering.hwt3: We're a young company with ambitious goals, and we focus on making things work.
engineering.notResearch: This is not a research environment, but it is a place for people who take ownership and build systems that actually get used.
engineering.working1: We're a small, international team — with people ranging from early 20s to mid-60s.
engineering.share1: we're excited about working at the edge of new technology
engineering.share2: we care about building something that matters
engineering.share3: we prefer doing it in a focused, low-friction environment
engineering.dtuNote: We're based at DTU in Copenhagen, with the flexibility to work from home. We trust people to manage their time, and expect them to take responsibility for what they build.
engineering.contactCta: If this sounds like the kind of environment you want to build in, reach out: roxane@twinspire.dk
